<!-- docgen:version=0.1.0 reason: first generation of this directory's README -->

## Purpose
This is the toolkit's safety-documentation directory; it owns the pre-install audit that lets a user (or Claude) verify the toolkit handles secrets correctly before wiring any hooks or daemons onto their machine.

## Files
- `SAFETY.md` — defines the audit a reader runs before installing: a four-point threat model (exfiltration, code execution on scanned content, hidden install behavior, path overreach) and five copy-pasteable `grep`/`cat`/`find` checks with their expected (mostly empty) results, plus a "when to refuse" rule that says stop the install if any check fails.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- The audit checks target sibling tool directories `secret-scrub/` and `docgen/` (the doc paths it greps are written `tools/secret-scrub/`, `tools/docgen/`) — keep its claims in sync with those: local-only network behavior, no `eval`/`exec` on scanned content, and the bounded write-path list (`.git/hooks/`, `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.secret-scrub.plist`, in-place redaction under the sessions dir, `<dir>/README.md`, `.docgen/state.json`).
- The root `README.md` links here as the "verify, not trust" safety story; changes to install scripts or write paths in either tool may invalidate the expected audit results documented here.

## Gotchas
- The grep paths in the audit are written as `tools/secret-scrub/` and `tools/docgen/`, but the actual tools live at the repo root (`secret-scrub/`, `docgen/`) — a reader running the commands verbatim from root will get no matches because the path is wrong, not because the code is clean. Adjust paths to the real layout if you update this file.
- The audit asserts specific safety invariants (no outbound network, no content `eval`, bounded write paths) — if you add a capability to `secret-scrub` or `docgen` that breaks one of these, this checklist becomes a false reassurance and must be updated in lockstep.
